description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, elevated array of solar panels supported by a metal framework. The panels are mounted above a platform that includes various equipment enclosures, electrical boxes, and what appears to be a tented or sheltered area on one side. The structure is set on a rectangular base with distinct zones marked by different colors and textures, possibly indicating operational or maintenance areas. The solar array is the dominant feature, with its grid-like pattern and metallic supports clearly visible. The installation is designed for outdoor use, likely in an urban or semi-urban environment, and is intended to generate and possibly store solar power. The overall design suggests a modular, possibly temporary or semi-permanent setup suitable for deployment in public spaces, parks, or utility areas.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a rectangular platform with equipment enclosures and a tented area, designed for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar array, tented area on the left, and various equipment boxes beneath the panels.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the wide solar panel surface, with equipment and colored zones visible beneath the array.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the tented area now on the right, showing the platform's layout and additional equipment.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the open platform area, steps, and the underside of the solar array.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly defined, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and posts
attribute_array_configuration: single large rectangular grid, approximately 4x6 panels
